blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I've been trying to figure out what to say since last night. I wrote it in my head a hundred different ways in the car.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You don't have to have it figured out.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I know. I just — I didn't want to mess it up.
Something about the way she said that. Like what she was about to say mattered enough to be afraid of.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Since the first day I noticed you.
I stopped. Not moving, not breathing — just stopped. Something in those words rearranged something.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I know that probably sounds like a lot. I know we barely — I know you don't know everything about me yet. But I'm already gone and if I don't say it now —
Her voice didn't break on that. It held. But barely.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: You were captivating to me from the beginning. I don't say things like that — you can ask anybody who knows me, I don't talk like that. But there's no other word for it. Every time you looked at me I felt it. Like you were actually seeing me.
I was sitting completely still in the dark. My whole apartment had gone quiet in a way that had nothing to do with the volume.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I used to find reasons to walk by. I would take the long way to class just to go past your hallway. I used to ask Jonathan about you.
She asked Jonathan. While I was on those steps trying to figure out how to say something. While I was in my head about timing and the right moment. She was already asking.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I kept thinking — he'll say something today. He'll say it this time on the steps. And then you said it. And then Wednesday happened and I thought —
She went quiet. Long enough that I could hear the street around her. An engine somewhere. A dog. Nothing I recognized.
